confirmed.title: LoginController Inertia props block references nonexistent route names and wrong prop keys
confirmed.evidence: auth.md (lines 66-73) documents LoginController passing props `'loginUrl' => route('arqel.auth.login')`, `'registerUrl' => route('arqel.auth.register')` ("null if registration disabled"), `'forgotPasswordUrl' => route('arqel.auth.password.request')`, and `'canResetPassword'`. The actual controller at packages/auth/src/Http/Controllers/LoginController.php:41-47 renders `arqel-dev/auth/Login` with `canRegister`, `canResetPassword`, `loginUrl => $panel?->getLoginUrl() ?? '/admin/login'`, `registerUrl => Route::has('register') ? route('register') : '/admin/register'`, `forgotPasswordUrl => $panel?->getForgotPasswordUrl() ?? '/admin/forgot-password'`. The route names `arqel.auth.login`, `arqel.auth.register`, and `arqel.auth.password.request` do NOT exist: packages/auth/src/Routes.php names them `login` (line 82), `register` (line 117), and `password.request` (line 186) — only the `.attempt` variants carry the `arqel.auth.` prefix (Routes.php:86,121). The doc also omits the `canRegister` prop and wrongly claims `registerUrl` is null when registration is disabled (code always returns a string).
confirmed.fix: Replace the props block to match LoginController::showForm(): keys `canRegister`, `canResetPassword`, `loginUrl` (from Panel::getLoginUrl()), `registerUrl` (route('register') fallback '/admin/register'), `forgotPasswordUrl` (from Panel::getForgotPasswordUrl()). Remove the fabricated `arqel.auth.*` route-name references.

verdict.reason:
CONFIRMED and NEW. Verified against source:

1. Route names (packages/auth/src/Routes.php): the GET routes are named `login` (line 82), `register` (line 117), and `password.request` (line 186). Only the POST/attempt variants carry the `arqel.auth.` prefix (`arqel.auth.login.attempt` line 86, `arqel.auth.register.attempt` line 121). Therefore the doc's route names `route('arqel.auth.login')`, `route('arqel.auth.register')`, and `route('arqel.auth.password.request')` (auth.md lines 68-70) reference route names that do NOT exist — calling route() with them would throw.

2. Prop keys/values (packages/auth/src/Http/Controllers/LoginController.php::showForm, lines 41-47): the controller renders `arqel-dev/auth/Login` with props `canRegister` (=> $panel?->registrationEnabled()), `canResetPassword` (=> $panel?->passwordResetEnabled()), `loginUrl` (=> $panel?->getLoginUrl() ?? '/admin/login'), `registerUrl` (=> Route::has('register') ? route('register') : '/admin/register'), `forgotPasswordUrl` (=> $panel?->getForgotPasswordUrl() ?? '/admin/forgot-password'). The doc omits the `canRegister` prop entirely and wrongly annotates `registerUrl` as "null if registration disabled" — the code always returns a string (route('register') or the '/admin/register' fallback), never null.

This is a genuine documentation-vs-code contradiction at apps/docs/reference/php/auth.md:66-73. It is NOT covered by any swept family: the R14 auth.md sweep addressed auth-page delivery status (AUTH-006/007/008 opt-in via Panel::login()/registration()/passwordReset(), arqel:doctor checkAuthStarterKit, forgot-password delivered), not the LoginController Inertia props block. New, distinct, actionable.

Fix: replace the props block at auth.md:66-73 to match showForm() — keys `canRegister`, `canResetPassword`, `loginUrl` (Panel::getLoginUrl() fallback '/admin/login'), `registerUrl` (route('register') fallback '/admin/register'), `forgotPasswordUrl` (Panel::getForgotPasswordUrl() fallback '/admin/forgot-password'); remove the fabricated arqel.auth.* route-name references and the incorrect "null if registration disabled" note.

Files: /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/apps/docs/reference/php/auth.md (lines 66-73); /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/auth/src/Http/Controllers/LoginController.php (lines 41-47); /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/auth/src/Routes.php (lines 82,86,117,121,186).

confirmed.title: Action form modal ("confirmation modal with fields") claimed unimplemented / Phase 2, but is shipped
confirmed.evidence: Line 425 states: "Arqel doesn't yet have Nova's built-in 'confirmation modal with fields' (planned Phase 2); for now, capture inputs via a custom React form and POST to the action endpoint." This is stale. The feature ships today: packages/actions/src/Concerns/HasForm.php defines form(array $fields), modalSize(), hasForm(); packages/actions/src/Action.php:365-366 serializes formFields + modalSize into the action schema; and packages-js/ui/src/action/ActionFormModal.tsx (exported from packages-js/ui/src/index.ts:10-11 as ActionFormModal) renders the field form inside the action modal, wired via ActionMenu.tsx. So an action with .form([...]) already produces exactly Nova's confirmation-modal-with-fields UX.
confirmed.fix: Rewrite pitfall #5 to state that Arqel supports action form modals via Action::form([...fields]) (+ modalSize()), rendered by @arqel-dev/ui's ActionFormModal; remove the "doesn't yet have" / "planned Phase 2" / custom-React-form workaround wording.

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED and NEW. apps/docs/guide/migration/from-nova.md:425 (pitfall #5) states: "Arqel doesn't yet have Nova's built-in 'confirmation modal with fields' (planned Phase 2); for now, capture inputs via a custom React form and POST to the action endpoint." This directly contradicts shipped code: (1) packages/actions/src/Concerns/HasForm.php defines form(array $fields), modalSize(string), modalWide(), hasForm(), getFormSchemaArray(), getFormValidationRules() etc.; (2) packages/actions/src/Action.php toArray() serializes 'form' => getFormSchemaArray(), 'formFields' => serializeFormFields($user), and 'modalSize' => getModalSize() into the action schema; (3) packages-js/ui/src/index.ts:10-11 exports ActionFormModal + ActionFormModalProps, and packages-js/ui/src/action/ActionMenu.tsx imports and renders <ActionFormModal> gated via actionNeedsGate (form.length > 0), passing fields from formFieldsByAction. So an action with .form([...]) already produces exactly Nova's confirmation-modal-with-fields UX. Not in any swept family (not a setter/signature, not a README Artisan list, not env/config/entry-point). Not a duplicate: the only prior from-nova.md ledger entry is line 4 (round 1, broken ./README.md link) — line 425 is an entirely distinct contradiction never touched. Actionable fix: rewrite pitfall #5 to state Arqel supports action form modals via Action::form([...fields]) + modalSize(), rendered by @arqel-dev/ui's ActionFormModal, and remove the "doesn't yet have"/"planned Phase 2"/custom-React-form workaround wording.

confirmed.title: "Planned next steps" section lists only delivered auth tickets and treats shipped work as future
confirmed.evidence: The section heading at line 260 is "## Planned next steps", yet lines 262-264 list AUTH-006 (login/logout), AUTH-007 (registration/email verification) and AUTH-008 (forgot-password/reset) each explicitly annotated "(delivered)". Line 266 then says "Once that ticket ships, `composer require arqel-dev/framework` + `php artisan arqel:install` will be enough" — describing already-shipped work as future. Code confirms delivery: packages/core/src/Panel/Panel.php exposes login() (l.77), registration() (l.98), passwordReset() (l.345) plus registrationFields()/passwordResetEnabled(). Nothing in this list is planned; the heading and the "Once that ticket ships" sentence are stale.
confirmed.fix: Rename the heading (e.g. "## Auth tickets — delivered") or move the delivered items out of a "Planned" section, and rewrite line 266 to present the composer require + arqel:install flow as the current state rather than a future "once that ticket ships" outcome.

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED and NEW/DISTINCT. The doc at apps/docs/guide/authentication.md:260 has a "## Planned next steps" heading whose three bullets (AUTH-006/007/008, lines 262-264) are each explicitly marked "(delivered)", and line 266 says "Once that ticket ships, `composer require arqel-dev/framework` + `php artisan arqel:install` will be enough" — framing already-shipped work as future. Source confirms delivery: packages/core/src/Panel/Panel.php exposes login() (l.77), registration() (l.98), passwordReset() (l.345), plus registrationEnabled()/passwordResetEnabled(). This is an internally incoherent artifact (heading "Planned" over items marked "delivered"). Although the ledger declares guide/authentication.md AUTH-006/007/008 SWEPT in R14, I verified via `git show 135a95e` that the R14 diff physically touched only the TL;DR, the "Why" heading, the comparison table, and the arqel:doctor section — it provably did NOT touch lines 258-266. The "## Planned next steps" block predates R14 (present in commit 5a069e4) and survived the sweep untouched, so these specific file+lines are a genuinely new, never-fixed contradiction, not a re-report of an already-edited passage. Actionable fix: rename the heading to reflect delivered status (e.g. "Auth tickets — delivered") and rewrite line 266 to describe the composer require + arqel:install flow as the current state rather than a future "once that ticket ships" outcome.
